name: LPL Financial
ai_strategy_summary: LPL Financial — the largest independent broker-dealer with 30,000+ advisors and ~8 million end clients — expanded its partnership with Anthropic in February 2026 to build Claude-powered AI tools for advisors. The timing was deliberate: the announcement came weeks after Altruist's Hazel launch triggered a 13% drop in LPL's stock and a $130B sell-off across the independent channel. Anthropic's new wealth management plug-ins give LPL (and other firms) building blocks to create private, compliance-controlled AI tools — customised to their advisors, powered by their data, controlled by their compliance teams. LPL chose a platform partner approach (Anthropic) rather than building from scratch, paralleling Wells Fargo's choice of Google Agentspace. The partnership puts LPL in the same infrastructure camp as Orion ($5.8T AUA, also partnering with Anthropic), creating a de facto Claude-powered layer across a significant share of the independent channel.
headline_metric: 30,000+ advisors; ~8M end clients; Anthropic/Claude partnership for compliance-controlled AI plug-ins
headline_initiative: Anthropic Claude plug-ins for advisor AI — private, compliance-controlled, built on firm data

advisor_productivity.headline: Anthropic Claude plug-ins for 30,000+ advisors — compliance-controlled, private, built on LPL data
advisor_productivity.detail: LPL's expanded Anthropic partnership provides building blocks for private AI plug-ins customised to LPL's advisor workflows, powered by LPL data, and controlled by compliance teams. Peter Nolan (Anthropic, Head of Asset & Wealth Management) described the approach: 'They own what they build. We're providing the building blocks.' This platform approach means LPL can iterate on AI capabilities without building AI infrastructure from scratch — but it also means LPL's AI timeline depends on Anthropic's financial services roadmap.
evidence: 30,000+ advisors, ~8M end clients (Fortune article, our intelligence entry)
Claude plug-ins: private, compliance-controlled, built on firm data (Anthropic LinkedIn post)
Announced same day as Anthropic's broader financial services plug-in launch with Orion (InvestmentNews)
Partnership came weeks after Hazel-triggered 13% stock drop (RIABiz)
